Dysphagia Knowledge Hub — 吞嚥困難知識庫

Dysphagia Nutrition Guide Collection

Malnutrition and dehydration are the most common complications of dysphagia. This section provides evidence-based guidance on maintaining adequate nutrition within texture-modified diet constraints — covering thickener selection, hydration protocols, protein optimization, oral nutritional supplements, and clinical malnutrition screening tools.

All Nutrition Guides

  • Hydration Strategies for Dysphagia Patients: Evidence, Thickened Fluids, and Frazier Free Water
  • Malnutrition in Dysphagia: Screening, Diagnosis, and Evidence-Based Management
  • Meal Planning for Dysphagia: Weekly Menus, Energy Density Strategies and IDDSI-Compliant Recipes
  • Micronutrient Deficiencies in Dysphagia Patients: A Complete Clinical Guide
  • Oral Nutritional Supplements (ONS) for Dysphagia: Choosing, Thickening and Integrating Into the Care Plan
  • Protein Optimization for Dysphagia Patients: Evidence-Based Strategies Within IDDSI Framework
  • Thickeners for Dysphagia: Starch vs Xanthan Gum, IDDSI Levels and Common Errors
  • Weight Management with Dysphagia: Preventing Malnutrition and Managing Overweight on Modified Textures
  • Weight Management in Dysphagia Patients: Preventing Malnutrition and Monitoring Progress
